subject: Social Science (History)
chapter: The Making of the National Movement: 1870s–1947

activities_projects.title: Timeline of the National Movement
activities_projects.objective: Understand the major events of the Indian national movement
steps: Draw a long horizontal line on chart paper
Mark important years from 1870 to 1947
Add key events such as formation of Indian National Congress, Non-Cooperation Movement, Civil Disobedience Movement and Quit India Movement
Decorate the timeline with small drawings or symbols
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the sequence of important events in the Indian freedom struggle
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy and presentation of the timeline (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Leaders of the Freedom Movement Chart
activities_projects.objective: Identify important leaders of the national movement
steps: Collect pictures of major leaders such as Mahatma Gandhi, Jawaharlal Nehru, Subhas Chandra Bose and Bal Gangadhar Tilak
Paste them on chart paper
Write two or three important contributions of each leader
Present the chart in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn about key leaders and their roles in the national movement
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of explanation and chart presentation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Moderates and Extremists Comparison
activities_projects.objective: Understand the difference between moderate and extremist approaches
steps: Draw a table with two columns titled Moderates and Extremists
List leaders belonging to each group
Write their methods of protest and political ideas
Discuss the differences with classmates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand different political strategies used during the freedom struggle

activities_projects.title: Poster on Swadeshi Movement
activities_projects.objective: Understand the importance of the Swadeshi Movement
steps: Create a poster promoting the use of Indian goods
Draw symbols such as spinning wheel or khadi cloth
Write slogans encouraging people to boycott foreign goods
Explain the message behind the poster
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the concept of Swadeshi and economic nationalism
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and explanation of Swadeshi ideas (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Role Play of a Freedom Movement Meeting
activities_projects.objective: Experience how political discussions happened during the national movement
steps: Divide students into groups
Assign roles such as Congress leader, journalist, or protester
Prepare short dialogues discussing British policies
Act out a meeting discussing freedom for India
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the debates and ideas of national leaders
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation and understanding of historical context (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Quit India Movement Discussion
activities_projects.objective: Learn about the significance of the Quit India Movement
steps: Read the section about the Quit India Movement
Write down the reasons for starting the movement
Discuss the slogan 'Do or Die'
Share findings with the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the importance of the Quit India Movement in the final phase of the struggle

activities_projects.title: Freedom Struggle Map Activity
activities_projects.objective: Locate important places related to the national movement
steps: Take a blank map of India
Mark places like Champaran, Dandi, Amritsar and Mumbai
Write the event associated with each place
Show the map to the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students connect historical events with geographical locations

activities_projects.title: Newspaper Report on a Freedom Protest
activities_projects.objective: Develop understanding of events through creative writing
steps: Imagine you are a journalist during the freedom struggle
Write a short newspaper report about a protest or movement
Include details such as date, place and leaders involved
Read the report aloud in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop historical imagination and writing skills

activities_projects.title: National Symbols and Unity Discussion
activities_projects.objective: Understand how symbols helped unite people during the movement
materials: Images of national flag, spinning wheel
steps: Observe different national symbols used during the freedom movement
Discuss their meanings and importance
Write a short paragraph about how symbols inspired people
Share the paragraph with classmates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the role of symbols in building national unity
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of explanation and understanding (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Independence Day Reflection Activity
activities_projects.objective: Reflect on the achievements of the national movement
steps: Write a short essay about India's independence in 1947
Mention the sacrifices made by freedom fighters
Explain why independence is important for the country
Share the essay in class discussion
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students appreciate the significance of India's independence and the struggle behind it
activities_projects.evaluation: Content and clarity of reflection (10 marks)
